titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 555

p.seg: Edgar king of the Scots to all people Scottish and English greeting.

Know that I give in alms to Almighty God and Saint Cuthbert my lord and to the church of Durham and the monks serving God in the same church now and for ever in the future, for the souls of my father and my mother, and for the salvation of my body and my soul, and my brothers' and sisters', and for all my ancestors and successors, the manor of Coldingham, and with this manor, these manors writtern below, that is, Old Cambus, Lumsdaine, Renton, Reston, Swinewood, Farndun, Ayton, the other Ayton, Prenderguest, Cramsmoo. These manors written above I concede to God and the saint aforesaid and to his monks, with all lands, woods, waters, tolls, shipwrecks, and all customs which pertain to the aforesaid manors, and which my father had quit and entire, to be freely disposed according to their own will in perpetuity.
